name: Cecil McBee
abstract: Cecil McBee (born May 19, 1935) is an American jazz bassist. He has recorded as a leader only a handful of times since the 1970s, but has contributed as a sideman to a number of classic jazz albums.

concerts.label: Laura Nyro, The Rascals,  Alice Coltrane, and Pharoah Sanders

about_quotes.speaker: Roy Haynes
about_quotes.evidence: I had a band with Wayne Shorter, Albert Dailey, and Cecil McBee.
about_quotes.doc_title: Roy Haynes — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Chico Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: I had joined Cecil McBee’s band... I was playing with his band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chico Freeman interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Tolliver
about_quotes.evidence: The only new person, new persons, was me and Cecil McBee. That’s his first time out. ... We all pile in those Checker Cabs and off to Rudy Van Gelder’s across the George Washington Bridge we’d go. And that first recording, I still think that’s one of the best recordings of Jackie and all of us on th

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Barron
about_quotes.evidence: Then, also on Enja Records, right after that, the next year, What If?, with Wallace Roney, John Stubblefield, Cecil McBee, and Victor Lewis... Yeah, one of mine too. That record, I had gotten a grant from the National Endowment to put together a group... That was the group.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Barron — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Tyner McCoy
about_quotes.evidence: the album, Blues for Coltrane actually got you the, you were awarded the Grammy for best jazz instrumental performance and that album included David Murray, Pharaoh Sanders, Cecil McBee, and Roy Haynes
about_quotes.doc_title: Tyner McCoy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: Just that thing we did yesterday with Cecil and Billy... I want to include Cecil... better rhythm section than this group
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
